metadata.recent_signal: Maya GenAI assistant launched Mar 2026; LLMCore platform actively being built; ET Dec 2025 feature on Myntra AI backbone
metadata.match_reason: Sr SWE Data Science explicitly mentions RAG, GraphRAG, agentic AI, vector DBs (Qdrant/Milvus/Pinecone/FAISS), Python+PySpark+Airflow. Maya GenAI initiatives.
metadata.cold_outreach_angle: Production RAG at 50K queries/month (Milvus), agentic LangGraph upgrade, and open-source LLM hosting map directly to their LLMCore + Maya GenAI platform work
metadata.notes: Perfect fit; Flipkart group company. Email pattern: firstname.lastname@myntra.com (49.7%). JD original posting expired; reconstructed from 4 sources. Company has 1K-5K employees, DS team ~296 LinkedIn members.

research_summary: Myntra (Flipkart Group, Bengaluru, 1K-5K employees) is building LLMCore -- a centralised GenAI platform -- and launched Maya, a real-time GenAI shopping assistant (Mar 2026, Aerospike-backed). The DS team role targets: RAG/GraphRAG, agentic AI, vector DBs (Milvus/Qdrant/FAISS), PySpark/Airflow, SLMs. Amit's production RAG at 50K queries/month (Milvus, BGE-M3, 45->95% MRR) and agentic LangGraph upgrade are a near-exact JD match. Email pattern firstname.lastname@myntra.com confirmed via RocketReach.
jd_full_text:
Myntra Data Science Team -- Senior Software Engineer / Senior Data ML Engineer
Location: Bengaluru | Experience: 4-8 years

About the team:
The Myntra Data Science team is at the forefront of AI/ML innovation, powering personalisation, fashion search, recommendation systems, and Myntra's Maya GenAI shopping assistant. We build LLMCore -- a centralised GenAI platform powering GenAI and Agentic AI use cases across Myntra.

Responsibilities:
- Design and build resilient, scalable, online metric-measurable ML/DL recommender APIs
- Build cost-effective yet scalable, ultra low latency production services
- Build services with open-sourced SLMs and computer vision models
- Experiment and deploy Agentic AI solutions
- RAG/GraphRAG/API retrieval pipeline implementation
- Work with vector databases: Qdrant, Milvus, Pinecone, FAISS
- Build and maintain data pipelines: PySpark, Airflow
- Collaborate with product and science teams to ship high-impact AI-powered features

Required:
- 4-8 years experience in ML engineering / data science
- Strong Python proficiency
- Experience with RAG/GraphRAG architectures and agentic AI systems
- Hands-on with vector DBs (Qdrant/Milvus/Pinecone/FAISS)
- PySpark/Airflow for data pipelines
- Production ML deployment experience
- Understanding of ML platform architecture and design

Nice to have:
- GenAI product experience (LLM-based applications)
- Open-source model serving (SLMs)
- Computer vision integration

(JD reconstructed from snippets across foundit.in, hiiiiiiring.com, instahyre, and tracker CSV signal. Original postings expired.)
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to mirror Myntra JD: RAG+agentic AI at scale, Milvus-backed retrieval (45% to 95% MRR), LangGraph agentic upgrade, on-premise SLM (Nemotron-70b). Technical Skills reordered: Vector DBs and Retrieval moved to top (JD names Milvus/Qdrant/Pinecone/FAISS explicitly), LLMs+Techniques second (RAG/Agentic AI/GraphRAG), Languages third (Python primary JD requirement). Added GraphRAG to LLMs line to match JD keyword. Vivansh bullets reordered: video course pipeline (Milvus+Cohere) moved before form builder since it shows vector DB usage. Projects swapped WeepScope (audio/hardware) for On-Premise OCR Pipeline (P5): JD mentions open-source SLMs and computer vision models; P5 shows Nemotron-70b self-hosted + DocTR vision OCR, directly matching both. Kept Voicer (P7) for Samsung Top 10 social proof. Hardware/IoT skills kept last since JD is pure software.

contacts.research_hook: 1 week ago shared 'dendrux' on LinkedIn -- async Python runtime for agents that survive failure and persist evidence. He's building LLMCore, Myntra's centralised GenAI platform for Agentic AI use cases. Active in solving agent fault-tolerance at production scale.
contacts.subject: saw your dendrux share -- quick question
contacts.content:
Hi Kumar,

Came across the dendrux share on your LinkedIn last week. The async Python runtime for agents that survive failure and persist evidence -- that problem has been sitting on my desk too. We built a LangGraph-based agentic RAG at Vivansh: 10+ platform APIs wired in, 2-stage permission-aware retrieval across 250+ user permissions, 500+ concurrent users. The moment an API call times out mid-graph, conversation state is gone, and we have patched around it rather than solved it at the platform layer.

Background: 2 years building production RAG and agentic systems. At Silver Touch I took a 50K-queries/month chatbot from 45% to 95% MRR after migrating FAISS to Milvus and benchmarking 6 embedding models. At Vivansh I rebuilt it as a LangGraph agent with 2 Milvus indexes and GPT-4o handling access control mid-graph.

If you are building LLMCore and solving agent fault-tolerance at the platform layer, I would be glad to hear how you approach it. Open to a 15-minute call next Tuesday or Thursday?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: saw your dendrux share -- quick question
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Kumar,

Bumping this in case it got buried. I dug into the dendrux repo a bit more since I sent this -- the evidence-persistence layer for agent state is genuinely useful for the fault-tolerance problem we have been dealing with at Vivansh. Curious whether LLMCore abstracts that at the platform layer or leaves it to individual agent implementations.

If now is not the right time, no worries.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: saw your dendrux share -- quick question
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Kumar,

Last note from me on this. If Myntra is not actively adding to the ML team right now, totally understood. Would still appreciate being on your radar for when that changes.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: LinkedIn bio: 'AI-Powered Scalable Systems, Distributed Architecture, Search, Recommendations & Content Platforms, Building High-Impact Teams'. Ex-MakeMyTrip. Manages the exact search/recommendation infrastructure this role would join.
contacts.subject: question about Myntra's retrieval stack
contacts.content:
Hi Yash,

Going through Myntra's engineering work this week and noticed your background -- AI-Powered Scalable Systems, distributed search, recommendation infra. That maps almost directly to what I have been building for the past year and a half.

At Silver Touch I ran the full vector DB evaluation for a 50K-queries/month RAG platform: Milvus vs Qdrant vs FAISS on production data. Milvus won by 10 MRR points. After adding BGE-M3 and a custom reranker, we went from 45% to 95% MRR. At Vivansh I took that retrieval stack into a LangGraph agentic system: 2-stage permission-aware filtering, 250+ access controls enforced per query, 500+ concurrent users on AWS EC2.

If the DS team at Myntra is growing on the ML engineering side, I would appreciate 15 minutes to hear what the retrieval and ranking challenges look like from your end. Open to a call next Tuesday or Thursday?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: question about Myntra's retrieval stack
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Yash,

Bumping this up. I put together a short note on how I would approach multi-tenant retrieval at scale given the real-time requirements Maya has -- happy to share it if useful context.

If the timing is off, no problem.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: question about Myntra's retrieval stack
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Yash,

Last one from me. If the ML team is not actively adding right now, I understand -- would still appreciate being on your radar for future openings.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Lead Scientist on Personalisation, Recommendations & Search -- the science lead for the DS team this role would join. His team directly owns the retrieval and ranking systems Amit's background maps to.
contacts.subject: question on personalisation retrieval at Myntra
contacts.content:
Hi Sangeet,

Was reading about Myntra's LLMCore platform and the Maya assistant and came across your role as Lead Scientist on personalisation, recommendations, and search. The retrieval side of that is close to what I have been building for the past 2 years.

At Silver Touch I ran a production benchmark of Milvus vs Qdrant vs FAISS on a 50K-queries/month platform. Milvus came out ahead by 10 MRR points on our data. After adding BGE-M3 and a custom reranker, MRR went from 45% to 95%. At Vivansh I extended that into a 2-stage LangGraph agentic system: a dedicated permission index for access control, then a content index across 10,000+ chunks with metadata filtering for course-level access. The MRR-driven evaluation and 2-stage architecture are the kinds of retrieval problems your team probably deals with on the search and recommendation side.

If there is an opening on the DS engineering side that fits this background, I would appreciate a pointer. If not, happy to be on your radar for when there is.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: question on personalisation retrieval at Myntra
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Sangeet,

Bumping this. I put my retrieval benchmark data into a short writeup this week -- Milvus vs Qdrant vs FAISS on production data with the methodology documented. Happy to share if it is useful context.

Amit
amitgohel.dev

contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Sangeet,

Last note from me. If now is not the right time, totally understood. Would appreciate being on your radar for future openings on the DS team.
